title: eDNA extraction: phenol-chloroform-isoamyl alcohol DNA purification from filters stored in Longmire buffer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bk9vh","text":"This is an organic DNA extraction method for filters preserved in 2 ml of Longmire buffer that uses a phase lock to allow easy decanting of the aqueous layer instead of pipetting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5bugt","text":"To make TlowE: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"73acf","text":"To make 50 ml","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3g3ub","text":"1. 500 µl of 1 M Tris-HCL, pH 8.0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"804dp","text":"2. 10 µl of 0.5 M EDTA, pH8.0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6dp9h","text":"3. up to 50 ml of nuclease free H2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"eou0e","text":"Phenol and Chloroform are very corrosive and irritant chemicals. Always perform these extractions in fume hoods, wear a lab coat, safety glasses, nitrile gloves and any other PPE. Also, always use resistant plasticware. Please refer to Safety Datasheets for more information. Phenol is volatile and can burn your skin and damage your eyes. Chloroform can make you faint and can even be lethal","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: heat-shock Filters in 2 mL of at 95 °C for 00:05:00, min and then allow samples to cool to room temperature Longmire lysis buffer by Ana Ramón-Laca
digestion add 100 µL proteinase K (final concentration 1 mg/mL )

phase lock set up add ca. 800 µL of vacuum grease with a syringe onto the wall of the 5ml tube without touching the lysate to avoid cross-contamination Eppendorf 5415R Refrigerated Centrifuge (Refrigerated Centrifuge, Eppendorf, EP-5415R, https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/private/ad988454678565d60fe90becdfcaa2e7bb2d68837cdf1a66e7e6147c0debe85e/ce49be3ax.jpg )
add 2 ml of phenol-chloroform-isoamyl (25:24:1) pH 8 pH is critical for good results
shake well and centrifuge 13300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
place ca. 800 µL of vacuum grease in 2 sets of empty tubes for CI. (These can be prefilled for convenience)

add 2.2 mL of chloroform:isoamyl 24:1 and decant aqueous layer from step 6. Important!! only add the chloroform just before use (while the tubes with PCI are in the centrifuge) so it does not affect the phase lock if grease is at the bottom.
shake well and centrifuge 13300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
add 2 mL of isopropanol (can be prefilled for convenience), add 80 µL of 5 Molarity (M) NaCl and decant aqueous layer from step 11
pour liquid off slowly and add 800 µL of ice cold 70 % (v/v) EtOH
shake well and centrifuge 13300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes and go to step #15
pour liquid off slowly and allow tubes to dry for 1 h or until dry
once they are dry, resuspend in 100 µL TE buffer (warm 37 °C to favor elution)
creator: Ana Ramón-Laca
